parsed_review.summary: CP00-528 is a clean, descriptor-only AI-governance (RP17.P04.M03.S09–S18) implementation-slice pack that passes independent read-only review. Pack binding (registry.js:239), requirements/deliverable distribution (registry.js:1342: security_audit 2, implementation 3, ui 2, fixture 1, test 1, hermes_evidence 1 = 10), descriptor/case-set builders (service.js:1849,1882), validators (validators.js:2650,2685), the contract projection (ai-governance-core-contract.json current_pack:16, descriptor:14078, requirements:2529, historical tail:14635), tests (model.test.js:975–1023), the plan pack (closeout-pack-plan.json:407), and next-pack-queue all agree with the stated baseline and with each other. The descriptor builders are pure frozen-object construction wrapped by freezeCp528Result, which stamps every no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data attestation; no child_process/fetch/fs-write/network/process.env appears in the source modules, so the no-write and no-real-data boundaries hold. Security/audit rows (permission_badge, audit_hint_display) remain descriptor-only with permission_decision_detail/audit_event_body/blocked_claim leak guards. The validate script (validate-rp17…mjs:346–819) deep-equals the stored contract projection against the freshly built descriptor and asserts coverage.valid, descriptor-only rows, handoff to CP00-529/S19, and that Claude is not promoted to final approval and local validation does not claim enterprise trust. Authority boundary is intact: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob), not the final approver; Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic review-packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. The pack does not overclaim — manifest production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false, so the production_ready gate is preserved rather than weakened. CP178+ hardening is satisfied: the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON (exit 0, 1436-byte stdout), and changed-file-scope correctly isolates unrelated dirty UI/workbook files. Downstream artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are appropriately absent because they are gated on this review. No P0/P1/P2/P3 defects were identified; the pack is clear to proceed to normalization, receipt validation, adjudication, construction inspection, final closeout validation, and commit.
